originalTask: Build Architecture 1 — Transcribe + Custom Vocabulary + Chart Agent. A medical spa POC where a provider taps Start Session, streams audio via WebSocket to Amazon Transcribe Streaming with Custom Vocabulary, sees live transcription, then on End Session the transcript goes to Bedrock Claude to extract structured chart fields into a JSON template. Charts and transcripts stored in DynamoDB/S3. Demo UI required.

subtasks.title: Terraform foundation: S3, DynamoDB, IAM roles
subtasks.description: Set up core AWS infrastructure with Terraform. Create: (1) S3 bucket for transcript/audio storage with server-side encryption, (2) DynamoDB table 'sessions' (partition key: sessionId) for raw transcripts, extracted charts, status, timestamps, (3) DynamoDB table 'chart-templates' (partition key: templateId) for chart template definitions, (4) DynamoDB table 'connections' (partition key: connectionId) for WebSocket connection tracking, (5) IAM roles for Lambda functions with least-privilege policies for Transcribe, Bedrock, S3, DynamoDB, API Gateway Management API access. Seed the chart-templates table with the Neuromodulator Treatment Form template JSON from the task.
verificationCriteria: terraform apply completes without errors
S3 bucket exists with encryption enabled
DynamoDB sessions table exists with correct key schema
DynamoDB chart-templates table exists and contains seeded Neuromodulator template
DynamoDB connections table exists with connectionId as partition key
IAM roles exist with correct policy attachments
All resources tagged with project=msi-transcribe-poc

subtasks.title: Custom Vocabulary for medical spa terminology
subtasks.description: Create an Amazon Transcribe Custom Vocabulary with medical spa terms: Botox, Dysport, Juvederm, Restylane, microneedling, dermaplaning, chemical peel, IPL, laser resurfacing, hyaluronic acid, platelet-rich plasma, PRP, subcutaneous, intramuscular, erythema, edema, contraindication, glabella, nasolabial, mentalis, orbicularis, corrugator, procerus, frontalis, masseter. Use a vocabulary table file (CSV) with Phrase, SoundsLike, IPA, DisplayAs columns. Deploy via Terraform or AWS CLI. Verify the vocabulary reaches READY state.
verificationCriteria: Custom Vocabulary exists in Transcribe with status READY
Vocabulary contains all required medical spa terms
Vocabulary name is stored in config/environment variable for Lambda to reference

subtasks.title: WebSocket API Gateway + connection management Lambdas
subtasks.description: Create an API Gateway WebSocket API with $connect, $disconnect, and sendaudio routes. Build Lambda functions for connection management: $connect stores connectionId + sessionId in DynamoDB connections table, $disconnect removes the record. The sendaudio route is wired to the streaming Lambda (subtask 4). Deploy via Terraform. Output the WebSocket URL (wss://) for the frontend.
verificationCriteria: WebSocket API deployed with wss:// endpoint
wscat can connect and disconnect without errors
$connect Lambda stores connectionId in DynamoDB connections table
$disconnect Lambda removes connectionId from DynamoDB connections table
CloudWatch logs confirm connection lifecycle

subtasks.title: Transcribe Streaming Lambda — real-time audio-to-text
subtasks.description: Build a single long-running Lambda function invoked by the sendaudio WebSocket route. Architecture: On the first sendaudio message (type=startSession), the Lambda opens a Transcribe Streaming session (StartStreamTranscription) with the Custom Vocabulary, then enters a loop — it reads audio chunks from the WebSocket (via API GW management API or callback URL), pipes them to Transcribe, and sends partial/final transcript results back to the client via API GW postToConnection. The Lambda stays alive for the entire session duration (up to 15min limit, fine for ~2min dictations). On endSession message, it closes the Transcribe stream, assembles the full transcript, stores it in S3, saves session metadata to DynamoDB, and exits. Key: this is ONE Lambda invocation per session, not one per message. Configure Lambda with 512MB memory and 900s timeout.
verificationCriteria: Send startSession via WebSocket — Lambda starts and opens Transcribe stream
Send audio chunks — receive partial transcript fragments back via WebSocket
Send endSession — receive full assembled transcript, Lambda exits cleanly
Full transcript stored in S3 bucket under sessions/{sessionId}/transcript.txt
Session record created in DynamoDB with status and transcript reference
Custom Vocabulary terms correctly transcribed (Botox, glabella, etc.)
Lambda CloudWatch logs show clean session lifecycle with no timeout errors

subtasks.title: Bedrock Claude chart extraction Lambda + HTTP API
subtasks.description: Build a Lambda function exposed via API Gateway HTTP API (POST /extract-chart). It accepts {sessionId, transcriptText, templateId}. It reads the chart template from DynamoDB chart-templates table, constructs a prompt with the transcript and template, calls Bedrock Claude (prefer claude-3-haiku for speed, fall back to sonnet) to extract structured fields. Implement dual-pass: first pass extracts fields, second pass reviews and scores confidence (0-1) per field. Store the extracted chart + confidence scores in DynamoDB sessions table. Return the filled chart JSON to the caller.
verificationCriteria: POST /extract-chart with sample transcript returns valid JSON matching chart template schema
All relevant chart fields populated from transcript content
Confidence scores (0-1) present for each extracted field
Chart record saved in DynamoDB sessions table
Medical spa terms correctly extracted (Botox units, treatment areas, injection sites)
Response time under 30 seconds for a typical 2-minute transcript

subtasks.title: Demo UI — interactive charting web page
subtasks.description: Build a single-page HTML/JS demo UI hosted on S3+CloudFront. Layout: (1) Header with MSI/Meevo branding and mode toggle (Live Session / Post-Session Dictation), (2) Left panel: Start/End Session buttons, live transcript area that shows text appearing in real-time, (3) Right panel: editable chart form that auto-populates after extraction, Save button. Mic capture via getUserMedia, encode to PCM 16-bit 16kHz mono, send chunks over WebSocket. On End Session, call POST /extract-chart, populate the form. Include a sample dictation script on the page for demo presenters. Include a dropdown to select chart template type (Neuromodulator pre-selected).
verificationCriteria: CloudFront URL loads the demo page with HTTP 200
Start Session activates microphone (browser permission prompt appears)
Live transcript text appears on screen while speaking
End Session triggers chart extraction and chart fields populate on the right
Chart fields are editable in the form
Save button persists corrections
Mode toggle switches between Live Session and Post-Session Dictation
Sample dictation script visible on the page
Works in Chrome

subtasks.title: DEMO-GUIDE.md — step-by-step demo walkthrough
subtasks.description: Write DEMO-GUIDE.md at /shared-repo/DEMO-GUIDE.md with: (1) Prerequisites (Chrome, microphone), (2) Demo URL (CloudFront), (3) Step-by-step walkthrough for a non-technical presenter to demo the full flow, (4) Sample dictation script to read aloud, (5) What to point out during the demo (live transcription, Custom Vocabulary accuracy, chart auto-population, confidence scores), (6) Troubleshooting tips (mic permissions, WebSocket connection issues), (7) Architecture diagram in text/ASCII showing the data flow.
verificationCriteria: DEMO-GUIDE.md exists at /shared-repo/DEMO-GUIDE.md
Contains working CloudFront demo URL
A non-technical person can follow the guide to run the demo end-to-end
Sample dictation script included
Troubleshooting section covers common issues
